children.heading: Approval by Secretary of plans
children.label: a Approval by Secretary of plans

children.text: Any State may apply to the Secretary for approval of a conservation plan.
Applications for the approval of conservation plans shall be made and reviewed by the Secretary in such manner as the Secretary shall by regulation prescribe.

children.label: i
children.text: meets the requirements set forth in , andsection 2903 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: is substantial in character and design; or

children.label: i
children.text: the plan does not meet the requirements set forth in , orsection 2903 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: to implement any part of the plan on the basis of the specifications, determinations, identifications, or priorities therein would threaten the natural stability and continued viability of any of the plan species concerned.

children.heading: Effect of approval of plans
children.label: b Effect of approval of plans

children.text: that portion of such plan that pertains to wildlife conservation shall be deemed to be an approved plan for purposes of (), commonly referred to as the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act [ et seq.]; andsection 6(a)(1) of the Act of September 2, 193716 U.S.C. 669e(a)(1)16 U.S.C. 669

that portion of such plan that pertains to fish conservation shall be deemed to be an approved plan for the purposes of [] commonly referred to as the Dingell-Johnson Sport Fish Restoration Act [ et seq.].section 6(a)(1) of the Act of August 9, 195016 U.S.C. 777e(a)(1)16 U.S.C. 777

children.heading: Conservation actions
children.label: c Conservation actions
children.text: If the Secretary approves the conservation plan of any State under subsection (a), those conservation actions set forth in the plan which pertain to nongame fish and wildlife shall be deemed to be eligible as nongame fish and wildlife projects for which reimbursement is available under .section 2905 of this title

children.heading: Nongame conservation actions in the absence of an approved plan
children.label: d Nongame conservation actions in the absence of an approved plan

children.text: are consistent with such of the requirements set forth in as may be appropriate, including, but not limited to, the requirements in paragraphs (3), (4), (5), and (7) of such section; andsection 2903 of this title
are substantial in character and design.
